rag_rules: Repair only unresolved property references and type mismatches that block analysis; do not modify unrelated architecture.
Delete or correct AGREE clauses containing unresolved identifiers or <error> types before attempting new declarations.
generic_rules: Fix root unresolved property or classifier references before addressing downstream type errors.
Ensure all AGREE expressions use only types int, real, or bool; never use AADL-type aliases like 'integer' or 'boolean'.
If a guarantee references an unresolved feature, remove it rather than introducing new bindings.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use 'int', 'real', 'bool' as scalar types in AGREE const/eq declarations—never 'integer', 'boolean', or custom aliases.
Compare only expressions of the same type: real with real, int with int; never mix types in binary expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new property definitions, packages, or type aliases to resolve references—only use what is declared in the model.
Avoid adding new annex clauses to fix unresolved references; first validate or delete invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
